outputs.main.sas:
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(lubridate)
library(haven)
library(metacore)
library(metatools)
library(xportr)

# Read SDTM datasets
lb <- read_xpt("sdtm/lb.xpt")
dm <- read_xpt("sdtm/dm.xpt")
vs <- read_xpt("sdtm/vs.xpt")

# Read ADSL
adsl <- read_xpt("adam/adsl.xpt")

# Filter LB for chemistry parameters
adlbc <- lb %>%
  filter(LBCAT == "CHEMISTRY")

# Merge with ADSL
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  derive_vars_merged(
    dataset_add = adsl,
    new_vars = exprs(TRTSDT, TRTEDT, TRT01P, TRT01PN, TRT01A, TRT01AN, 
                     AGE, AGEGR1, AGEGR1N, RACE, RACEN, SEX, 
                     SAFFL, ITTFL, EFFFL)
  )

# Derive analysis date and day
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  derive_vars_dt(
    new_vars_prefix = "A",
    dtc = LBDTC
  ) %>%
  derive_vars_dy(
    reference_date = TRTSDT,
    source_vars = exprs(ADT)
  )

# Assign PARAMCD and PARAM
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    PARAMCD = LBTESTCD,
    PARAM = LBTEST,
    PARCAT1 = LBCAT,
    PARCAT1N = 1
  )

# Derive AVAL from LBSTRESN
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    AVAL = LBSTRESN,
    AVALC = LBSTRESC
  )

# Derive ANRLO and ANRHI
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    ANRLO = LBSTNRLO,
    ANRHI = LBSTNRHI
  )

# Derive ANRIND (Analysis Reference Range Indicator)
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    ANRIND = case_when(
      is.na(AVAL) ~ NA_character_,
      is.na(ANRLO) & is.na(ANRHI) ~ NA_character_,
      AVAL < ANRLO ~ "LOW",
      AVAL > ANRHI ~ "HIGH",
      TRUE ~ "NORMAL"
    )
  )

# Derive baseline flag
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  restrict_derivation(
    derivation = derive_var_extreme_flag,
    args = params(
      by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD),
      order = exprs(ADT, LBSEQ),
      new_var = ABLFL,
      mode = "last"
    ),
    filter = !is.na(AVAL) & ADT <= TRTSDT
  )

# Derive BASE and BNRIND
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  derive_var_base(
    by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD),
    source_var = AVAL,
    new_var = BASE,
    filter = ABLFL == "Y"
  ) %>%
  derive_var_base(
    by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD),
    source_var = ANRIND,
    new_var = BNRIND,
    filter = ABLFL == "Y"
  )

# Derive CHG and PCHG
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  derive_var_chg() %>%
  derive_var_pchg()

# Derive SHIFT variable
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    SHIFT1 = case_when(
      is.na(BNRIND) | is.na(ANRIND) ~ NA_character_,
      TRUE ~ paste(BNRIND, "TO", ANRIND)
    )
  )

# Derive analysis visit variables
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    AVISIT = case_when(
      VISITNUM == 1 ~ "Baseline",
      VISITNUM == 3 ~ "Week 2",
      VISITNUM == 4 ~ "Week 4",
      VISITNUM == 5 ~ "Week 8",
      VISITNUM == 6 ~ "Week 12",
      VISITNUM == 7 ~ "Week 16",
      VISITNUM == 8 ~ "Week 20",
      VISITNUM == 9 ~ "Week 24",
      VISITNUM == 10 ~ "Week 26",
      TRUE ~ VISIT
    ),
    AVISITN = case_when(
      VISITNUM == 1 ~ 0,
      VISITNUM == 3 ~ 2,
      VISITNUM == 4 ~ 4,
      VISITNUM == 5 ~ 8,
      VISITNUM == 6 ~ 12,
      VISITNUM == 7 ~ 16,
      VISITNUM == 8 ~ 20,
      VISITNUM == 9 ~ 24,
      VISITNUM == 10 ~ 26,
      TRUE ~ VISITNUM
    )
  )

# Derive analysis flags
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    ANL01FL = if_else(!is.na(AVAL) & AVISITN > 0, "Y", NA_character_)
  )

# Add treatment variables
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    TRTP = TRT01P,
    TRTPN = TRT01PN,
    TRTA = TRT01A,
    TRTAN = TRT01AN
  )

# Derive DTYPE for LOCF
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  mutate(
    DTYPE = NA_character_
  )

# Select and order final variables
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  select(
    STUDYID, USUBJID, SUBJID,
    TRTP, TRTPN, TRTA, TRTAN,
    AGE, AGEGR1, AGEGR1N, RACE, RACEN, SEX,
    SAFFL, ITTFL, EFFFL,
    PARAMCD, PARAM, PARCAT1, PARCAT1N,
    AVAL, AVALC, BASE, CHG, PCHG,
    ANRLO, ANRHI, ANRIND, BNRIND, SHIFT1,
    AVISIT, AVISITN, ADT, ADY,
    ABLFL, ANL01FL, DTYPE,
    LBSEQ, VISITNUM, VISIT, LBDTC, LBDY
  )

# Apply variable labels
var_labels <- c(
  STUDYID = "Study Identifier",
  USUBJID = "Unique Subject Identifier",
  SUBJID = "Subject Identifier for the Study",
  TRTP = "Planned Treatment",
  TRTPN = "Planned Treatment (N)",
  TRTA = "Actual Treatment",
  TRTAN = "Actual Treatment (N)",
  AGE = "Age",
  AGEGR1 = "Pooled Age Group 1",
  AGEGR1N = "Pooled Age Group 1 (N)",
  RACE = "Race",
  RACEN = "Race (N)",
  SEX = "Sex",
  SAFFL = "Safety Population Flag",
  ITTFL = "Intent-to-Treat Population Flag",
  EFFFL = "Efficacy Population Flag",
  PARAMCD = "Parameter Code",
  PARAM = "Parameter",
  PARCAT1 = "Parameter Category 1",
  PARCAT1N = "Parameter Category 1 (N)",
  AVAL = "Analysis Value",
  AVALC = "Analysis Value (C)",
  BASE = "Baseline Value",
  CHG = "Change from Baseline",
  PCHG = "Percent Change from Baseline",
  ANRLO = "Analysis Normal Range Lower Limit",
  ANRHI = "Analysis Normal Range Upper Limit",
  ANRIND = "Analysis Reference Range Indicator",
  BNRIND = "Baseline Reference Range Indicator",
  SHIFT1 = "Shift from Baseline to Post-Baseline",
  AVISIT = "Analysis Visit",
  AVISITN = "Analysis Visit (N)",
  ADT = "Analysis Date",
  ADY = "Analysis Relative Day",
  ABLFL = "Baseline Record Flag",
  ANL01FL = "Analysis Flag 01",
  DTYPE = "Derivation Type",
  LBSEQ = "Sequence Number",
  VISITNUM = "Visit Number",
  VISIT = "Visit Name",
  LBDTC = "Date/Time of Specimen Collection",
  LBDY = "Study Day of Specimen Collection"
)

adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  set_variable_labels(.labels = var_labels)

# Sort dataset
adlbc <- adlbc %>%
  arrange(STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD, AVISITN, ADT)

# Write XPT file
write_xpt(adlbc, "adam/adlbc.xpt", label = "Analysis Dataset Lab Blood Chemistry")
